findings.title: EDINET sweep still applies no document-type filter — an interim (quarterly) filing's balance-sheet instant can be booked as the annual figure (c2 F13 / c3 F3, unresolved)
findings.statement: list_documents requests all filings for a day with type=2 and no docTypeCode/ordinanceCode filter (edinet.py:197-200), and _period takes a balance instant from any context whose period resolves (edinet.py:59-63) with no guard that the instant belongs to an annual securities report (有価証券報告書). Income/cashflow are protected by the full-year duration check (edinet.py:64-69), but balance instants are NOT, so a quarterly/semi-annual report's period-end instant can be booked as the annual balance. EDINET is opt-in (requires a Subscription-Key, edinet.py:200) so blast radius is limited, but the values are silently wrong when it is enabled.

evidence.note: list_documents — type=2 only, no docTypeCode filter for annual securities reports
_period balance branch returns the instant with no annual-report guard (contrast the income/cashflow full-year check just below)
period taken from any resolving context, incl. interim

findings.failureScenario: A JP company's Q2 report lands in the swept day; its mid-year balance instant is booked as the annual TotalAssets/Equity, distorting every balance-derived ratio for that symbol.
findings.recommendation: Filter list_documents (or the sweep loop) to the annual securities report docTypeCode (120) and/or require the balance instant to match the annual period end; add a fixture with an interim filing asserting it is skipped.

findings.title: EDINET context parsing still drops the consolidated/non-consolidated dimension — a parent-only (単体) figure can be booked for the group (c2 F14 / c3 F4, unresolved)
findings.statement: parse_xbrl_instance records only start/end/instant from each context (edinet.py:88-93) and never the explicit member that marks 連結 (Consolidated) vs 単体 (NonConsolidated). When both a consolidated and a non-consolidated context exist for the same concept and period, the winner is decided purely by concept rank / first-writer (edinet.py:114-119) with no preference for the consolidated member, so a parent-only figure can be booked as the group's audited value. Opt-in (Subscription-Key) so limited blast radius.

evidence.note: context loop captures only start/end/instant — the Consolidated/NonConsolidated member is discarded
rank/first-writer tiebreak with no consolidated-context preference

findings.failureScenario: A group whose non-consolidated (単体) Revenue context is parsed before the consolidated (連結) one books parent-only revenue as the audited group figure.
findings.recommendation: Capture the consolidated/non-consolidated member per context and prefer the consolidated one (or drop non-consolidated when a consolidated exists); add a fixture with both contexts asserting the consolidated value wins.

findings.title: ingest/enrichment.py remains the top hotspot (617 LOC, 7 run_* cycles in one module) — c2 F1 / c3 F5 opportunity still open
findings.statement: The audited enrichment cycles all still live in one module: enrichment.py is 617 LOC holding 7 parallel run_* cycles (ESEF enrichment.py:43, EDGAR :131, EDGAR-bulk :214, FSDS :301, Companies House :396, EDINET :437, ESEF-sweep :513). The per-cycle contracts are already clean, so each could move beside its provider (or an ingest/cycles/ package) to stop one file growing with every new source.

evidence.note: run_esef_cycle — first of 7 run_* cycles sharing one 617-LOC module

findings.recommendation: Move each run_<source>_cycle next to its provider (or an ingest/cycles/ package), keeping the shared GLEIF/heartbeat helpers in one place.
